sections.title: Cybersecurity Program
sections.text: Each Covered Entity shall maintain a cybersecurity program designed to protect the confidentiality, integrity and availability of the Covered Entity's Information Systems.

sections.title: Cybersecurity Policy
sections.text: Based on its Risk Assessment, each Covered Entity shall design its Cybersecurity Policy to perform the following core cybersecurity functions: identify, protect, detect, respond, and recover.

sections.title: Chief Information Security Officer
sections.text: Each Covered Entity shall designate a qualified individual responsible for overseeing and implementing the Covered Entity's cybersecurity program and enforcing its cybersecurity policy.

sections.title: Penetration Testing and Vulnerability Assessments
sections.text: Based upon its Risk Assessment, each Covered Entity shall conduct annual penetration testing and bi-annual vulnerability assessments.

sections.title: Audit Trail
sections.text: Each Covered Entity shall maintain systems to reconstruct material financial transactions and detect cybersecurity events. Audit logs shall be retained for a period of at least five years.

sections.title: Access Privileges
sections.text: Each Covered Entity shall limit user access privileges to Information Systems that provide access to Nonpublic Information and shall periodically review such access privileges.

sections.title: Application Security
sections.text: Each Covered Entity shall utilize procedures to evaluate, assess, or test the security of externally developed applications utilized as part of the Covered Entity's Information Systems.

sections.title: Risk Assessment
sections.text: Each Covered Entity shall conduct periodic risk assessments that inform the design of its cybersecurity program and update the risk assessment when there are material changes to its business or technology environment.

sections.title: Cybersecurity Personnel and Intelligence
sections.text: Each Covered Entity shall utilize qualified cybersecurity personnel and update the cybersecurity program based on current cybersecurity threat intelligence.

sections.title: Third Party Service Provider Security Policy
sections.text: Each Covered Entity shall implement policies and procedures designed to ensure the security of Information Systems and Nonpublic Information accessible to third party service providers.

sections.title: Multi-Factor Authentication
sections.text: Each Covered Entity shall utilize Multi-Factor Authentication to protect against unauthorized access to Nonpublic Information or Information Systems.

sections.title: Limitations on Data Retention
sections.text: Each Covered Entity shall include policies and procedures for the secure disposal of Nonpublic Information within a reasonable time after it is no longer needed for business operations or legal purposes.

sections.title: Training and Monitoring
sections.text: Each Covered Entity shall implement regular cybersecurity awareness training for all personnel and maintain procedures to monitor for authorized users' activity on Information Systems.

sections.title: Encryption of Nonpublic Information
sections.text: Each Covered Entity shall encrypt Nonpublic Information held or transmitted by the Covered Entity both in transit and at rest, or shall utilize effective alternative compensating controls reviewed and approved by the CISO.

sections.title: Incident Response Plan
sections.text: Each Covered Entity shall establish an incident response plan designed to promptly respond to cybersecurity events and restore normal operations.

sections.title: Notice to Superintendent
sections.text: Each Covered Entity shall notify the superintendent as promptly as possible but in no event later than 72 hours from a determination that a cybersecurity event has occurred that is required to be reported.
